summary: Iteration 16 — WhatsApp Media Inbox feature end-to-end verified. Backend: 13/13 pytest passing on live Meta Cloud API. Covered — (1) POST /api/whatsapp/send-media with 1x1 PNG returned 200 with {id, media_kind:'image', provider_message_id, gridfs_id, size:67}, wallet debited exactly 40 paise, timeline includes media field with type/mime_type/gridfs_id/size/filename, (2) GET /api/media/{gridfs_id} — SA fetches any, owning tenant fetches own, other tenant gets 403, invalid ObjectId returns 400, (3) wallet — insufficient balance → 402 'Insufficient wallet balance', valid balance → 40 paise debit, refund on Meta failure verified via debit+credit rows in wallet_transactions, (4) file size — 26 MB upload → 413, empty file → 400, (5) MIME auto-detect — image/png→image, application/pdf→document (Meta accepted PDF in this run so kind='document' asserted), (6) regression — /api/whatsapp/send-message text and template paths still work, (7) /api/wallets auto-creates wallet rows for newly created companies so freshly created TEST_IT16_A/B tenants appear in the SA list with balance_paise=0. Frontend: SA logged in → opened contact 'Rajeev' (+919711623561) → WhatsApp channel tab → free-form mode → wa-attach-media-button visible → clicked hidden wa-media-file-input → 1x1 PNG selected → wa-media-pending panel showed filename+size+image thumb → wa-media-clear removed pending state and restored the attach button. Send button correctly relabels to 'Send media' when a file is pending.

issues: Pending file shows '0 KB' for the 67-byte PNG (Math.round(size/1024) rounds sub-KB to 0). Cosmetic — display '<1 KB' or the raw byte count for tiny files.
Console warning: repeated WSS connect-close on /api/ws (harmless; useRealtime.js reconnect timer fires during route mounts). Already flagged in iter 15.

action_items: OPTIONAL: In ContactProfile.jsx pending panel, guard against sub-KB rounding to 0 — show '<1 KB' for files <1024 bytes.

OPTIONAL: /api/whatsapp/send-media accepts file.filename fallback 'upload.<ext>' but ext parsing splits mime_type not filename — a JPEG uploaded without a name becomes 'upload.jpeg' which is fine, but a wildcard mime like 'application/octet-stream' becomes 'upload.octet-stream'. Consider using filename extension when available.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 3266 lines — still growing (media routes added). Extract /media + /whatsapp/send-media + wallet code into routers/ modules (previously flagged in iter 15, still open).

In whatsapp_send_media (server.py:2813), the file is uploaded to GridFS BEFORE the wallet check. If a user with insufficient balance repeatedly attempts to send media, GridFS orphans accumulate (wallet_debit at line 2835 rejects but the file at 2814 is already written). Move wallet check ABOVE the media_fs.upload_from_stream call OR add cleanup on 402.

Media MIME detection trusts client-supplied file.content_type verbatim — a client can send bytes of an .exe with content_type='image/png' and it will be uploaded as an image. Consider using python-magic or checking magic bytes for security-sensitive tenants.

GET /api/media/{gridfs_id} — SA bypass (user.get('company_id') falsy) is correct but there is no audit log entry for cross-tenant reads by SA. For compliance, add audit('media_view', ...) on that path.

download_status='pending' state is initialized only in _meta_handle_inbound (not shown in current diff); make sure inbound media messages set 'media.download_status':'pending' on insert so the UI Loader2 spinner shows before the background task completes.
test_credentials: SA admin@cpaas.io / Admin@12345. Ephemeral tenants TEST_IT16_A_<ts>, TEST_IT16_B_<ts> auto-cleaned via SA DELETE /companies/{id}.
seed_data_creation: Two ephemeral tenants (A + B) plus a wallet top-up of 1000-1400 paise on tenant A per test class; test PNG (67 bytes) constructed inline via base64.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Media inbox feature is fully live. Meta Cloud API accepts the 1x1 test PNG and the test PDF (2 sends charged so far during this test run). Test number +919999999999 is accepted by the sandbox WABA (wamid returned). GridFS bucket 'wa_media' — files stored with metadata {company_id, mime_type, source:'outbound_upload'|'meta_inbound', size, created_at}. /api/media/{gridfs_id} auth-scoped: SA any, tenant only own. 402 flow returns before GridFS upload? — NO, GridFS upload happens FIRST then wallet debit (potential orphan on 402, code-review flagged). Test file test_iteration16_media_inbox.py is reusable regression suite. Frontend flow: contact page → WhatsApp tab → freeform → wa-attach-media-button → wa-media-file-input (hidden) → wa-media-pending → wa-media-clear. Timeline renders MediaPreview via authed blob-fetch (responseType:'blob'). Inbound media tests skipped (no way to trigger Meta webhook without real inbound message).
rca of the issue: No functional bugs found. Two orphan-risk / hardening comments only. Feature works end-to-end on live Meta creds.
